title: chenopodium × fallax
intro: Chenopodium × fallax is a hybrid plant species in the Amaranthaceae family, resulting from the cross between the Chenopodium album and Chenopodium rubrum. It is also known as fall beet or fat hen, and is commonly found in Europe, North America, and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and has been used to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Chenopodium × fallax, such as infusions and tinctures, are often used to support immune function and promote overall health and well-being.
